# Martian Research Infrastructure Documentation
*IMPORTANT: This project has mostly been generated by gemini flash 3.0 thinking*


**Project:** `research-thin-server`  
**Author:** Yutaro  
**Host Machine:** Proxmox VE (PVE) Homelab Topology  

---

## 1. Architectural Overview

The application architecture utilizes an immutable "Build Locally, Deploy Remote" design pattern. This isolates the runtime environment from compilation overhead, optimizes disk space on production nodes, and ensures absolute data privacy via localized TLS termination.

### Network & Data Flow Topology

```text
[ Public Client Browser ]
           │
           │ (HTTPS / Port 443 - Fully Encrypted TLS Tunnel)
           ▼
 [ Cloudflare Nameservers ]  ◄─── Set to "Grey Cloud" (DNS Only Mode)
           │                      *Acts purely as DNS directory.
           │                      *Cannot decrypt or intercept payloads.
           ▼
[ PVE Host / Nginx LXC Container ] (Reverse Proxy & SSL Termination Node)
           │
           │ * Holds Let's Encrypt Wildcard (*.martian17.com) Keys Locally
           │ * Decrypts TLS payload natively
           │
           │ (HTTP Proxy Pass via Internal PVE Network Bridge)
           ▼
[ PVE Host / Docker VM (192.168.1.100:8081) ] (Minimal Debian Guest Node)
           │
           │ (Docker Virtual Network Bridge Interface)
           ▼
[ Axum Container: research-thin-server (Internal Port 8080) ]
           │
           ▼ (Volume Mounted Endpoint)
   [ VirtioFS Shared Target Folder: /mnt/research ]

```

### Build & Delivery Pipeline

```text
[ Local Laptop (elitebook64) ] 
  ├── Native Arch Linux Environment
  ├── Compiles Cargo dependencies using BuildKit
  └── Exports immutable image layer to compressed tarball
           │
           ▼ (Secure Transport Layer)
  [ SSH Gateway / Proxy Jump (root@homelab.martian17.com) ]
           │
           ▼ (Internal LAN Drop)
[ Target Docker VM Host (/opt/research-thin-server/) ]
  └── Ingests tarball directly into Docker Engine cache via `docker load`

```

---

## 2. Component Configurations

### Component A: Local Build Environment (Laptop)

The local build layer leverages native host hardware to handle high-performance Rust builds.

#### `~/.dockerignore`

Prevents local development artifacts, target caches, and staging configuration files from bleeding into the engine tracking context.

```text
target/
.git/
.gitignore
Dockerfile
docker-compose.yml
research-thin-server.tar.gz

```

#### `/etc/docker/daemon.json`

Configures the local Docker daemon to use BuildKit permanently across all build invocations.

```json
{
  "features": {
    "buildkit": true
  }
}

```

#### `Dockerfile`

A highly optimized multi-stage container specification using pinning baselines to guarantee `glibc` compatibility with Debian runtimes.

```dockerfile
# --- Stage 1: Build Environment ---
FROM rust:1.95-slim AS builder
WORKDIR /usr/src/app
COPY . .
RUN cargo build --release

# --- Stage 2: Minimal Runtime Environment ---
FROM debian:13-slim
WORKDIR /app

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y ca-certificates && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=builder /usr/src/app/target/release/research-thin-server /app/prod_binary
RUN chmod +x /app/prod_binary

ENTRYPOINT ["/app/prod_binary"]

```

---

### Component B: Deployment Host Configuration (Docker VM)

The destination runtime server remains entirely unpolluted by source files or cargo build caches.

#### Local Orchestration Shortcut (`~/.ssh/config` on Laptop)

```text
Host homelab-gate
    HostName homelab.martian17.com
    User root

Host docker-vm
    HostName 192.168.1.100
    User root
    ProxyJump homelab-gate

```

#### `/opt/research-thin-server/docker-compose.yml`

```yaml
services:
  research-thin-server-service:
    image: research-thin-server:latest
    container_name: research-thin-server-container
    ports:
      - "8081:8080"
    volumes:
      - /mnt/research:/app/content
    restart: unless-stopped

```

---

### Component C: Reverse Proxy Layer (Nginx LXC)

The entry point routing node handles absolute encryption duties using an automated DNS-01 token challenge mechanism.

#### Cloudflare Secret Access File (`~/.secrets/certbot/cloudflare.ini`)

```ini
dns_cloudflare_api_token = YOUR_SCOPED_DNS_EDIT_TOKEN

```

#### Centralized Cryptographic Snippet (`/etc/nginx/snippets/wildcard-ssl.conf`)

Shares unified root and subdomain keys transparently across any future servers without code replication.

```nginx
ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/[martian17.com/fullchain.pem](https://martian17.com/fullchain.pem);
ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/[martian17.com/privkey.pem](https://martian17.com/privkey.pem);

ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:DHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:DHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384';
ssl_session_cache shared:SSL:10m;
ssl_session_timeout 1d;
ssl_session_tickets off;

```

#### Production Site Proxy Specification (`/etc/nginx/conf.d/research.conf`)

```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    listen [::]:80;
    server_name research.martian17.com;
    return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
}

server {
    listen 443 ssl;
    listen [::]:443 ssl;
    server_name research.martian17.com;

    include snippets/wildcard-ssl.conf;

    location / {
        proxy_pass [http://192.168.1.100:8081](http://192.168.1.100:8081);
        
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;

        # WebSocket Transport Matrix Mapping
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
    }
}

```

---

## 3. Active Delivery Script (Local Execution)

Run this sequence of execution steps from the project root directory (`~/prog/research-thin-server`) on your local Arch Laptop to trigger a live sync modification update:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Step 1: Compiling Immutable Target via Local BuildKit ==="
docker build -t research-thin-server:latest .

echo "=== Step 2: Compressing Target Layer Matrix to Tarball ==="
docker save research-thin-server:latest | gzip > research-thin-server.tar.gz

echo "=== Step 3: Preparing Target Workspace Structure over Proxy Jump ==="
ssh docker-vm "mkdir -p /opt/research-thin-server"

echo "=== Step 4: Shipping Orchestration Core and Binary Layers ==="
scp research-thin-server.tar.gz docker-compose.yml docker-vm:/opt/research-thin-server/

echo "=== Step 5: Hot Reloading Remote Container Stack ==="
ssh docker-vm "
  cd /opt/research-thin-server && \
  gzip -dc research-thin-server.tar.gz | docker load && \
  rm research-thin-server.tar.gz && \
  docker compose up -d
"

echo "=== Step 6: Cleaning Local Temp Archives ==="
rm research-thin-server.tar.gz

echo "=== Live Deployment Completed Successfully ==="

```

---

## 4. Future Workflow for "Site #2"

Because the global wildcard configuration certificate maps seamlessly to `*.martian17.com` and your public records use a wildcard `*` target, scaling up additional distinct apps takes exactly 10 seconds. You do not need to rerun Certbot or interact with Let's Encrypt servers.

### To add an entirely new application (e.g., `metrics.martian17.com` running on port 3000):

**1. Create a brand new configuration block inside the Nginx LXC container:**

```bash
vim /etc/nginx/conf.d/metrics.conf

```

**2. Paste this short template block:**

```nginx
server {
    listen 443 ssl;
    server_name metrics.martian17.com;
    
    # Imports the existing certificates instantly with zero validation overhead
    include snippets/wildcard-ssl.conf; 

    location / {
        proxy_pass [http://192.168.1.100:3000](http://192.168.1.100:3000);
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
    }
}

```

**3. Test and reload the Nginx daemon:**

```bash
nginx -t && systemctl reload nginx

```

The new subdomain goes live instantly across the internet with flawless, verified end-to-end cryptographic SSL encryption.
